en: As he passed by, he saw a man blind from birth.
zh: 耶穌走路的時候，看見一個生下來就瞎眼的人。

en: And his disciples asked him, "Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?"
zh: 他的門徒問他：“拉比，這人生下來就瞎眼，是誰犯了罪？是他呢，還是他的父母呢？”

en: Jesus answered, "It was not that this man sinned, or his parents, but that the works of God might be displayed in him.
zh: 耶穌回答：“不是他犯了罪，也不是他的父母犯了罪，而是要在他身上彰顯 神的作為。

en: We must work the works of him who sent me while it is day; night is coming, when no one can work.
zh: 趁著白晝，我們必須作那差我來者的工；黑夜一到，就沒有人能作工了。

en: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world."
zh: 我在世上的時候，是世界的光。”

en: Having said these things, he spat on the ground and made mud with the saliva. Then he anointed the man's eyes with the mud
zh: 說了這話，就吐唾沫在地上，用唾沫和了一點泥，把泥抹在瞎子的眼睛上，

en: and said to him, "Go, wash in the pool of Siloam" (which means Sent). So he went and washed and came back seeing.
zh: 對他說：“你去西羅亞池洗一洗吧。” 於是他就去了，洗完了，走的時候，就看見了。

en: The neighbors and those who had seen him before as a beggar were saying, "Is this not the man who used to sit and beg?"
zh: 那時，鄰居和以前常常見他討飯的人說：“這不是那一向坐著討飯的人嗎？”

en: Some said, "It is he." Others said, "No, but he is like him." He kept saying, "I am the man."
zh: 有的說：“是他。”有的說：“不是他，只是像他。”他自己說：“是我。”

en: So they said to him, "Then how were your eyes opened?"
zh: 他們就問他：“你的眼睛是怎樣開的呢？”

en: He answered, "The man called Jesus made mud and anointed my eyes and said to me, 'Go to Siloam and wash.' So I went and washed and received my sight."
zh: 他回答：“那名叫耶穌的人和了一點泥，抹在我的眼上，對我說：‘你去西羅亞池洗一洗吧。’我去一洗，就看見了。”

en: They said to him, "Where is he?" He said, "I do not know."
zh: 他們說：“那人在哪裡？”他說：“我不知道。”

en: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had formerly been blind.
zh: 他們就把那個從前瞎眼的人帶到法利賽人那裡。

en: Now it was a Sabbath day when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.
zh: 耶穌和了泥開他眼睛的那一天，正是安息日。

en: So the Pharisees again asked him how he had received his sight. And he said to them, "He put mud on my eyes, and I washed, and I see."
zh: 法利賽人又問他是怎樣可以看見的。他告訴他們：“耶穌把泥抹在我的眼上，我一洗就看見了。”

en: Some of the Pharisees said, "This man is not from God, for he does not keep the Sabbath." But others said, "How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?" And there was a division among them.
zh: 有幾個法利賽人說：“那個人不是從 神那裡來的，因為他不守安息日。”另外有些人說：“一個罪人怎能行這樣的神蹟呢？”他們就起了紛爭。

en: So they said again to the blind man, "What do you say about him, since he has opened your eyes?" He said, "He is a prophet."
zh: 他們再對瞎子說：“他既然開了你的眼睛，你說他是甚麼人？”他說：“他是個先知。”

en: The Jews did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight, until they called the parents of the man who had received his sight
zh: 猶太人不信他從前是瞎眼，現在才能看見的，於是把他的父母叫來，

en: and asked them, "Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?"
zh: 問他們：“這是你們所說那生下來就瞎眼的兒子嗎？現在他怎麼又能看見呢？”

en: His parents answered, "We know that this is our son and that he was born blind.
zh: 他的父母回答：“我們知道他是我們的兒子，生下來就瞎眼；

en: But how he now sees we do not know, nor do we know who opened his eyes. Ask him; he is of age. He will speak for himself."
zh: 現在他是怎樣可以看見的，我們不知道；誰開了他的眼睛，我們也不知道。你們問他吧；他已經長大成人，可以替自己講話了。”

en: (His parents said these things because they feared the Jews, for the Jews had already agreed that if anyone should confess Jesus to be Christ, he was to be put out of the synagogue.)
zh: 他的父母這樣說，是因為怕猶太人，原來猶太人已經定好了，不論誰承認耶穌是基督，就要把那人趕出會堂。

en: Therefore his parents said, "He is of age; ask him."
zh: 因此他的父母說：“他已經長大成人，你們問他吧。”

en: So for the second time they called the man who had been blind and said to him, "Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner."
zh: 於是法利賽人第二次把那從前瞎眼的人叫來，對他說：“你應當歸榮耀給 神，我們知道這人是個罪人。”

en: He answered, "Whether he is a sinner I do not know. One thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see."
zh: 那人回答：“他是不是個罪人，我不知道；我只知道一件事，就是我本來是瞎眼的，現在能看見了。”

en: They said to him, "What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?"
zh: 他們就問：“他向你作了甚麼呢？他怎樣開了你的眼睛呢？”

en: He answered them, "I have told you already, and you would not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become his disciples?"
zh: 他回答：“我已經告訴你們，但是你們不聽；為甚麼現在又想聽呢？你們也想成為他的門徒嗎？”

en: And they reviled him, saying, "You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
zh: 他們就罵他，說：“你才是他的門徒，我們是摩西的門徒。

en: We know that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this man, we do not know where he comes from."
zh: 我們知道 神曾對摩西說話；只是這個人，我們不知道他從哪裡來。”

en: The man answered, "Why, this is an amazing thing! You do not know where he comes from, and yet he opened my eyes.
zh: 那人對他們說：“這就奇怪了，他開了我的眼睛，你們竟然不知道他從哪裡來。

en: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but if anyone is a worshiper of God and does his will, God listens to him.
zh: 我們知道 神不聽罪人的祈求，只聽那敬畏 神，遵行他旨意的人。

en: Never since the world began has it been heard that anyone opened the eyes of a man born blind.
zh: 自古以來，沒有人聽過生下來就是瞎眼的，有人可以開他們的眼睛。

en: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing."
zh: 這人若不是從 神那裡來的，他就不能作甚麼。”

en: They answered him, "You were born in utter sin, and would you teach us?" And they cast him out.
zh: 他們說：“你的確是在罪中生的，還敢教訓我們嗎？”就把他趕出去。

en: Jesus heard that they had cast him out, and having found him he said, "Do you believe in the Son of Man?"
zh: 耶穌聽見他們把他趕出去，後來遇見他的時候，就對他說：“你信人子嗎？”

en: He answered, "And who is he, sir, that I may believe in him?"
zh: 他說：“先生，誰是人子，好讓我信他呢？”

en: Jesus said to him, "You have seen him, and it is he who is speaking to you."
zh: 耶穌說：“你已經見過他，現在跟你說話的就是他。”

en: He said, "Lord, I believe," and he worshiped him.
zh: 那人說：“主啊，我信。”就向他下拜。

en: Jesus said, "For judgment I came into this world, that those who do not see may see, and those who see may become blind."
zh: 耶穌說：“我到這世上來是為了審判，使那看不見的能夠看見，能看見的反而成了瞎眼的。”

en: Some of the Pharisees near him heard these things, and said to him, "Are we also blind?"
zh: 有些和耶穌在一起的法利賽人聽了這話，就說：“難道我們也是瞎眼的嗎？”

en: Jesus said to them, "If you were blind, you would have no guilt; but now that you say, 'We see,' your guilt remains.
zh: 耶穌對他們說：“如果你們是瞎眼的，就沒有罪了；但現在你們說‘我們能看見’，所以你們還是有罪的。”
